summary: The wind ensemble (also called concert band, symphonic band, or military band) is a large instrumental ensemble consisting of woodwinds, brass, and percussion — without a string section. It is distinct from the orchestra and the marching band. Key figures include conductor Frederick Fennell (founder of the Eastman Wind Ensemble) and march composer John Philip Sousa.

works.name: What is the Wind Ensemble
works.description: The wind ensemble (also called concert band, symphonic band, military band, or wind band) consists of woodwinds, brass, and percussion — no strings. It is different from the orchestra and the marching band. Conductor Frederick Fennell founded the Eastman Wind Ensemble at the Eastman School of Music, which became a landmark for interpreting the genre. John Philip Sousa ('The March King') led a famous wind ensemble for decades. The Leeds Music Corporation commissioned works for this ensemble, including Milhaud's Suite Française. The genre includes both 'concert' settings (with symphonies, suites, and tone poems) and marching band formats.

clues.clue: John Philip Sousa led one for decades; composed marches like 'The Stars and Stripes Forever,' 'The Thunderer,' and 'Semper Fidelis'
examples: This ensemble is scored for pieces like 'The Thunderer' and 'Semper Fidelis,' which were composed by a leader of one of these ensembles named John Philip Sousa.
John Philip Sousa led this type of ensemble, for which he composed marches like 'The Stars and Stripes Forever.'

works.name: Gustav Holst — Suites for Military Band
works.description: Holst wrote two suites for military/wind band — the First Suite in E-flat and the Second Suite in F. The Second Suite incorporates seven English folk songs including 'I'll Love My Love,' 'Greensleeves,' and 'Fantasia on the Dargason' (the final movement). The First Suite opens with a chaconne. The 'Song of the Blacksmith' in one suite calls for an anvil. Holst originally composed the Fantasia on the Dargason for the second suite. Wikipedia

clues.clue: Two suites for military/wind band: First in E-flat (opens with a chaconne) and Second in F (incorporates English folk songs including 'Greensleeves' and ends with 'Fantasia on the Dargason')
examples: Gustav Holst originally composed his 'Fantasia on the Dargason' for the second of his two suites for this ensemble, which are in E-flat and F.
A chaconne opens Gustav Holst's first suite for this ensemble.

clues.clue: 'Song of the Blacksmith' movement calls for an anvil; precedes 'Fantasia on the Dargason'
examples: One of these pieces calls for an anvil in its third movement 'Song of the Blacksmith,' which precedes the 'Fantasia on the Dargason.'
A piece for this ensemble calls for an anvil in its 'Song of the Blacksmith' movement before its rapid 'Fantasia on the Dargason.'

works.name: Percy Grainger — Lincolnshire Posy and Other Works
works.description: Percy Grainger composed Lincolnshire Posy for wind band — a six-movement piece based on English folk tunes, including 'Rufford Park Poachers' and 'The Lost Lady Found' (final movement). He also composed Irish Tune and Colonial Song for band. Lincolnshire Posy features unusual time signatures including 1.5/4 and 2.5/4 in the 'Lord Melbourne' movement. Wikipedia

clues.clue: Lincolnshire Posy — six-movement suite based on folk tunes including 'Rufford Park Poachers' and 'The Lost Lady Found'; unusual time signatures (1.5/4, 2.5/4) in 'Lord Melbourne'
examples: Percy Grainger composed Lincolnshire Posy for this ensemble.
One suite for this instrumentation is based on six folk tunes, including 'Rufford Park Poacher.'

clues.clue: Also wrote Colonial Song and Irish Tune for band
examples: Colonial Song and Irish Tune are commonly played pieces for this ensemble by the composer of Lincolnshire Posy, Percy Grainger.

works.name: Berlioz — Grande Symphonie funèbre et triomphale
works.description: Hector Berlioz wrote a 'grande' symphony for wind ensemble (symphonie funèbre et triomphale) for the tenth anniversary of the July Revolution. The martial third movement, 'Apotheosis,' can optionally include a chorus. Wikipedia

clues.clue: Berlioz's Grande Symphonie funèbre et triomphale — for wind ensemble; written for the tenth anniversary of the July Revolution; optional chorus in the third movement 'Apotheosis'
examples: A 'grande' piece in this genre subtitled 'funebre et triomphale' was written by Hector Berlioz.
an optional chorus appears in the martial third movement 'Apotheosis' of a piece in this genre written for the tenth anniversary of the July Revolution.

works.name: Other Notable Works for Wind Ensemble
works.description: Other significant wind ensemble repertoire includes: Paul Hindemith's Symphony in B-flat (his only work for the medium, with a fugue marked 'Rather broad' as the third movement); Ralph Vaughan Williams's English Folk Song Suite and a suite quoting 'Dives and Lazarus' and 'Seventeen Come Sunday'; William Schuman's George Washington Bridge and Chester Overture; David Maslanka's A Child's Garden of Dreams and Give Us This Day; Frank Ticheli's Angels in the Architecture (begins and ends with a soprano singing 'I am an angel of light'); John Mackey's Wine-Dark Sea (three movements inspired by the Odyssey); Johan de Meij's 'Lord of the Rings' Symphony; Handel's Music for the Royal Fireworks; and Alfred Reed's Armenian Dances.

clues.clue: Hindemith's Symphony in B-flat — his only work for wind ensemble; third movement is a fugue marked 'Rather broad'
examples: Paul Hindemith's only work for this instrumentation was his Symphony In B-Flat

The English Folk Song Suite was originally written for this kind of ensemble.

examples: This genre does not usually include singers, but one piece in this genre begins and ends with a soprano singing 'I am an angel of light,' that piece is Angels in the Architecture by Frank Ticheli.

comprehensive_summary:
The wind ensemble (also called concert band, symphonic band, military band, or wind band) is an instrumental ensemble consisting of woodwinds, brass, and percussion without a string section. It differs from the orchestra (which has strings) and the marching band (which is mobile and primarily ceremonial). The conductor Frederick Fennell founded the landmark Eastman Wind Ensemble at the Eastman School of Music. John Philip Sousa, 'The March King,' led a famous wind ensemble for decades and composed iconic marches such as 'The Stars and Stripes Forever,' 'The Thunderer,' and 'Semper Fidelis.'

Gustav Holst wrote two suites for military band: the First Suite in E-flat (which opens with a chaconne) and the Second Suite in F (which incorporates seven English folk songs including 'Greensleeves' and concludes with the 'Fantasia on the Dargason'). The 'Song of the Blacksmith' movement calls for an anvil. Percy Grainger's Lincolnshire Posy is a six-movement suite based on English folk tunes including 'Rufford Park Poachers' and 'The Lost Lady Found'; it features unusual time signatures (1.5/4 and 2.5/4) in the 'Lord Melbourne' movement. Grainger also wrote Irish Tune and Colonial Song for band.

Hector Berlioz wrote the Grande Symphonie funèbre et triomphale for wind ensemble to commemorate the tenth anniversary of the July Revolution; the martial third movement 'Apotheosis' can optionally include chorus. Paul Hindemith's Symphony in B-flat is his only work for the medium; its third movement is a fugue marked 'Rather broad.' Ralph Vaughan Williams's English Folk Song Suite (originally for military band, published by Boosey & Hawkes) quotes 'Dives and Lazarus' and 'Seventeen Come Sunday' in its first movement. Handel's Music for the Royal Fireworks was originally written for wind ensemble to accompany fireworks.

Other significant works include: Frank Ticheli's Angels in the Architecture (unusual for the genre in beginning and ending with a soprano singing 'I am an angel of light'); John Mackey's Wine-Dark Sea (three movements inspired by the Odyssey); David Maslanka's A Child's Garden of Dreams and Give Us This Day; William Schuman's George Washington Bridge and Chester Overture; Alfred Reed's Armenian Dances; and Johan de Meij's 'Lord of the Rings' Symphony. The Leeds Music Corporation commissioned Darius Milhaud's Suite Française for this ensemble.
